name: Urwa ibn Battat
title: Leader of Killers
shortDescription: A violent commander of the Kufan army who played a role in surrounding Abbas and martyring Suwayd ibn Amr.
fullStory:
Urwa ibn Battat al-Tha'labi was another notorious executioner in Ibn Sa'd's army in Karbala. He was among those willing to commit any crime to gain favor with Ibn Ziyad.

On Ashura, he was present in several key scenes. Urwa was one of the commanders whose soldiers surrounded Abbas (AS) near the Euphrates and showered him with arrows and sword strikes.

Also, when Suwayd ibn Amr (the last living wounded companion of the Imam) rose again to fight with a dagger upon hearing of Imam Hussain's (AS) martyrdom, Urwa ibn Battat and Zayd ibn Ruqad attacked and oppressedly martyred him.

Urwa's end, like the other Karbala killers, was dark; after Mukhtar's uprising, he fled to Basra with Shabath ibn Rib'i and joined Mus'ab ibn al-Zubayr to stay safe.
quoteArabic: فَعَطَفَ عَلَيْهِ عُرْوَةُ بْنُ بَطّاطٍ التَّغْلِبِيُّ... فَقَتَلُوهُ...
quote: (Maqtal report:) Then Urwa ibn Battat and Zayd ibn Ruqad attacked him (Suwayd) and killed him...
